sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Larry, presents with a 2-year history of chronic back pain that started after moving furniture.
The pain is described as a sharp stabbing pain that radiates down to the right leg, including the big toe, and is exacerbated by sitting.
He also experiences numbness and tingling in the right leg, particularly at the foot.
He denies any loss of sensation in the genital or rectal area and no weakness or loss of bladder or bowel control.
He has tried physical therapy, pain medications, and has been considered for surgery, but would like to avoid it.
His day is affected by the back pain, which has led to weight gain.
He has a history of playing golf, which has been impacted by the back pain.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of smoking, which he quit 20 years ago.
He does not recall any family members with spine conditions.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The musculoskeletal back exam reveals midline tenderness at L4-L5 disk space with right-sided lumbar paravertebral tenderness.
The patient experiences pain with lumbar flexion and a positive straight leg test.
The neurological examination shows decreased sensation to light touch at the right lateral thigh at L4 dermatome.
The patellar and Achilles reflexes are symmetrical.
Test Results: The MRI results show disk herniation with nerve impingement.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an L4-L5 herniated disk with radiculopathy.
Problem list: Herniated disk L4-L5; Radiculopathy; Chronic lumbar back pain; Weight gain.
Plan:
I prescribed methylprednisolone 80mg IM for the lumbar injection.
I will inject the corticosteroid into the lumbar spine under fluoroscopy.
I will monitor the patient's response to the injection and schedule follow-up appointments as needed.
The patient will not require any ongoing treatment, but may need additional injections in the future.
Follow-up: The patient will return in 1-2 weeks for follow-up and to assess the response to the injection.
